You'll Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now? — React Basics: Class? Hooks? — (3)
You'll Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now?

Function Component? Class Component? React Hooks? What even are these things? In this post we'll get to know the two ways of writing React and what makes them different. We'll convert the same counter from a Class Component into a Function Component, and then walk through the two rules you have to follow when using React Hooks.
You'll Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now? — React Basics: Hello React (2)

We've already taken a quick look at how React differs from Vue in terms of syntax. So now it's time to actually start getting to know React. In this post we'll set up a React environment with just a CDN, render our first "Hello React", and peek at how JSX and React.createElement relate under the hood.
You'll Have to Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now? - React Basics - What Even Is React? - (1)
You'll Have to Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now?

No matter what, you've got to start by getting comfortable with React's basic concepts and a bit of its background. That groundwork makes it way easier to spot the differences when you pick up other frameworks later. I'm a Vue developer myself, so I'm writing this with the mindset of someone adding one more framework to the toolbox — which means you'll see a few side-by-side comparisons between Vue and React syntax. Mostly I want to make this an easy on-ramp for fellow Vue devs. Enough preamble, let's get into it.
What Is the Dunning–Kruger Effect? Thought You Knew It, But Actually Didn't

The Dunning–Kruger effect is a cognitive bias where the less you know, the more confident you feel. This post walks through the four classic stages — Mount Stupid, the Valley of Despair, the Slope of Enlightenment and the road to mastery — and why beginners hit them so hard.
Why I Recommend Engineers Write a Blog

A lot of us run into landmines and bugs while learning and developing — and those experiences are gold. If you don't record them, that's a waste, not to mention the chance you'll hit the same problem again. I really recommend blogging — let me explain why.
BetterDiscord: How to Customize and Power Up Your Discord

Discord keeps blowing up — even companies are dropping Slack for it. But sometimes the default UI just isn't enough, and you want a more comfortable, customised setup. This post walks through using BetterDiscord to customise and enhance your Discord on macOS.
What the Hell Is .DS_Store? Should You Commit It to Git?

What actually is .DS_Store? Should it go in your Git repo? Why does it even exist? This post walks through what .DS_Store is, why every macOS folder has one, and why you should keep it out of version control.
VSCode 1.69 Broke `! + Tab`? Here's the Fix
After VSCode updated to 1.69, the classic `! + tab` HTML expansion stopped working. Here's how to get it back.
Making VSCode's Tailwind CSS IntelliSense Work With React JSX
Tailwind CSS IntelliSense worked fine in a CRA (Create React App) setup, but for some reason after I moved to Vite for React it stopped working. This post is just a note on how I fixed it.
Installing CRA Throws "You are running create-react-app, which is behind the latest release" — Here's the Fix

I needed an older version of Create React App for some testing, but kept hitting the "you are running create-react-app, which is behind the latest release" error. This post is just a quick note on how I got around it.
